title: Neolemaireocereus aragonii
taxonomy: Neolemaireocereus aragonii is classified within the family Cactaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Neolemaireocereus and is native to Mexico. The species name aragonii refers to the Aragon region in Mexico. Neolemaireocereus aragonii is further classified as a species within the subfamily Pereskioideae. It is a cactus species that is relatively rare and has limited cultivation.

The plant's extracts have shown to exhibit antioxidant and cytotoxic effects, which may be beneficial in treating various health conditions. intro: Neolemaireocereus aragonii, commonly known as Aragon cactus or Aragon pincushion, is a species of cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce pain, inflammation, and oxidative stress in the body. The bioactive constituents of Neolemaireocereus aragonii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Neolemaireocereus aragonii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin irritations, and gastrointestinal issues.
